sentences.quote: We report what we believe to be the first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in Lean~4; we have not located a comparable Berry-phase-gate formalization in the Isabelle/HOL AFP, Coq (QuantumLib / QWIRE), or Agda quantum libraries, and invite correction if prior work exists.
sentences.quote_normalized: we report what we believe to be the first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in lean~4; we have not located a comparable berry-phase-gate formalization in the isabelle/hol afp, coq (quantumlib / qwire), or agda quantum libraries, and invite correction if prior work exists.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedged precedence claim with explicit caveat ('we believe', 'invite correction'). Verifiable in principle only via systematic AFP/Coq/Agda search; not mechanically resolvable. Soft hedge accepted.

sentences.quote: The target is the doublon geometric SWAP gate recently demonstrated by Kiefer~\textit{et~al.} (Nature~2026, arXiv:2507.22112) in a Fermi-Hubbard dimer with dynamical optical lattices.
sentences.quote_normalized: the target is the doublon geometric swap gate recently demonstrated by kiefer~et~al. (nature~2026, arxiv:2507.22112) in a fermi-hubbard dimer with dynamical optical lattices.
sentences.agent_notes: Kiefer2026 in CITATION_REGISTRY; arXiv:2507.22112 matches registry entry.

sentences.quote: Working entirely within Lean~4 + Mathlib, we formalize the exact $3\times 3$ singlet-sector Hamiltonian, its charge-conjugation (BDI) symmetry protection, the explicit closed-form spectrum and dark state, the geometric SWAP operator as a real-Householder reflection $\USWAP = I - (2/\text{gap}^2)\,|d\rangle\langle d|$, and the two necessary finite-dimensional conditions for a Berry-phase interpretation of the gate action: the dark state acquires a $-1$ sign on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle, and the Hamiltonian expectation value vanishes pointwise along the zero-energy path (so the integrated dynamical phase vanishes for any continuous extension).
sentences.quote_normalized: working entirely within lean~4 + mathlib, we formalize the exact $3\times 3$ singlet-sector hamiltonian, its charge-conjugation (bdi) symmetry protection, the explicit closed-form spectrum and dark state, the geometric swap operator as a real-householder reflection $\uswap = i - (2/gap^2)\,|d\rangle\langle d|$, and the two necessary finite-dimensional conditions for a berry-phase interpretation of the gate action: the dark state acquires a $-1$ sign on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle, and the hamiltonian expectation value vanishes pointwise along the zero-energy path (so the integrated dynamical phase vanishes for any continuous extension).
sentences.agent_notes: Meta-summary of paper deliverables; each named result resolves to a Lean theorem.

sentences.quote: We also ship the Tier-2 algebraic scaling theorem that distinguishes direct exchange (linear in $U$) from superexchange ($4t^2/U$) with an explicit remainder bound.
sentences.quote_normalized: we also ship the tier-2 algebraic scaling theorem that distinguishes direct exchange (linear in $u$) from superexchange ($4t^2/u$) with an explicit remainder bound.
sentences.agent_notes: Both theorems exist in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: The complete formalization lives in a single module (\texttt{FermiHubbardDimer.lean}), consists of \fhdTotal{}~theorems (across the 19 internal sections), with zero sorry, zero new axioms, and a Python cross-validation layer covering every theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: the complete formalization lives in a single module (fermihubbarddimer.lean), consists of \fhdtotal{}~theorems (across the 19 internal sections), with zero sorry, zero new axioms, and a python cross-validation layer covering every theorem.
sentences.agent_notes: \fhdTotal in counts.tex = 143; grep '^theorem ' FermiHubbardDimer.lean = 143 (also 0 'lemma '). Sorry count = 0. Project axiom count = 1 (gapped_interface_axiom in SPTClassification.lean) — paper qualifies as 'zero new axioms' which is correct in scope of this formalization.

sentences.quote: The deliberate scope boundary is Layer~2 of the deep-research target hierarchy: all results are finite-dimensional linear algebra over Mathlib's existing \texttt{Matrix}, \texttt{EuclideanSpace}, and \texttt{OrthonormalBasis} API.
sentences.quote_normalized: the deliberate scope boundary is layer~2 of the deep-research target hierarchy: all results are finite-dimensional linear algebra over mathlib's existing matrix, euclideanspace, and orthonormalbasis api.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-boundary statement; consistent with rest of paper.

sentences.quote: Full adiabatic holonomy (continuous parameterized eigenvector bundles, Berry connection, Kato's theorem) is explicitly deferred to future work.
sentences.quote_normalized: full adiabatic holonomy (continuous parameterized eigenvector bundles, berry connection, kato's theorem) is explicitly deferred to future work.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope disclaimer; matches Section 6 (Target B) and Conclusion.

sentences.quote: The formalization demonstrates that the Kiefer \textit{et~al.} gate mechanism is not, at its core, a fact about connections on line bundles---it is the observation that $\cos(\pi) = -1$ composed with an explicit rank-1 Householder reflection on a parameterized real symmetric $3\times 3$ matrix.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization demonstrates that the kiefer et~al. gate mechanism is not, at its core, a fact about connections on line bundles---it is the observation that $\cos(\pi) = -1$ composed with an explicit rank-1 householder reflection on a parameterized real symmetric $3\times 3$ matrix.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive summary; the constituent claims (cos(pi)=-1 sign flip via W8a; rank-1 Householder reflection via U_SWAP_singlet) are formalized.

sentences.quote: Berry phases underlie one of the most experimentally compelling classes of quantum gates: \emph{geometric} gates, whose unitary action depends only on the topology of a closed path in parameter space, not on the traversal speed or duration~\cite{Berry1984,Zanardi1999}.
sentences.quote_normalized: berry phases underlie one of the most experimentally compelling classes of quantum gates: geometric gates, whose unitary action depends only on the topology of a closed path in parameter space, not on the traversal speed or duration~.
sentences.agent_notes: Both bibkeys in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: Geometric gates promise intrinsic robustness against control-pulse fluctuations---a property that has motivated sustained experimental effort from superconducting qubits~\cite{Leek2007} through trapped ions and neutral atoms to, most recently, dynamical optical lattices.
sentences.quote_normalized: geometric gates promise intrinsic robustness against control-pulse fluctuations---a property that has motivated sustained experimental effort from superconducting qubits~ through trapped ions and neutral atoms to, most recently, dynamical optical lattices.
sentences.agent_notes: Leek2007 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: In April~2026, Kiefer~\textit{et~al.}~\cite{Kiefer2026} reported the first neutral-atom realization of a geometric SWAP gate in a Fermi-Hubbard dimer.
sentences.quote_normalized: in april~2026, kiefer~et~al.~ reported the first neutral-atom realization of a geometric swap gate in a fermi-hubbard dimer.
sentences.agent_notes: Kiefer2026 in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: The experiment uses ${}^{40}$K atoms in a dynamical two-site superlattice, and drives a closed loop in the $(t, \Delta)$ control-parameter plane (tunnel coupling, inter-well bias).
sentences.quote_normalized: the experiment uses ${}^{40}$k atoms in a dynamical two-site superlattice, and drives a closed loop in the $(t, \delta)$ control-parameter plane (tunnel coupling, inter-well bias).
sentences.agent_notes: Restatement of cited experiment.

sentences.quote: At the end of the loop, the dark state of the singlet sector---which remains at zero energy for the entire cycle---has acquired a $-1$ sign, implementing \textbf{SWAP} on the computational subspace $\{\ket{\uparrow\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow\uparrow}\}$ with loss-corrected amplitude fidelity $\sim 99.9\%$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at the end of the loop, the dark state of the singlet sector---which remains at zero energy for the entire cycle---has acquired a $-1$ sign, implementing swap on the computational subspace $\{\ket{\uparrow\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow\uparrow}\}$ with loss-corrected amplitude fidelity $\sim 99.9\%$.
sentences.agent_notes: The 99.9% fidelity is reported from Kiefer2026; not independently recomputed here. Verifiable only by reading source.

sentences.quote: The robustness is striking: the gate is $4\times$ less sensitive to lattice imperfections than the comparable superexchange gate~\cite{Kiefer2026}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the robustness is striking: the gate is $4\times$ less sensitive to lattice imperfections than the comparable superexchange gate~.
sentences.agent_notes: Quantitative robustness claim cited to source. The paper's Section 4 separately discusses a derivative-ratio (factor-of-2) interpretation.

sentences.quote: This paper formalizes the algebraic core of the Kiefer~\textit{et~al.} mechanism in the Lean~4 proof assistant~\cite{Lean4,Mathlib}.
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper formalizes the algebraic core of the kiefer~et~al. mechanism in the lean~4 proof assistant~.
sentences.agent_notes: Both bibkeys in CITATION_REGISTRY. Note: revtex4-2 natbib emits a forward-reference 'undefined on first pass' warning (cosmetic, resolves on rerun).

sentences.quote: The target is deliberately scoped: we formalize the finite-dimensional matrix content---the Hamiltonian, the dark state, the SWAP operator, the sign flip, the dynamical-phase-vanishes statement---without touching the adiabatic theorem, fiber bundles, or connections on vector bundles.
sentences.quote_normalized: the target is deliberately scoped: we formalize the finite-dimensional matrix content---the hamiltonian, the dark state, the swap operator, the sign flip, the dynamical-phase-vanishes statement---without touching the adiabatic theorem, fiber bundles, or connections on vector bundles.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope statement consistent with formalization.

sentences.quote: The result is the \emph{first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in Lean~4}; we have not located comparable prior work in the Isabelle/HOL AFP, Coq (QuantumLib / QWIRE), or Agda quantum libraries.
sentences.quote_normalized: the result is the first end-to-end formalization of a geometric quantum gate in lean~4; we have not located comparable prior work in the isabelle/hol afp, coq (quantumlib / qwire), or agda quantum libraries.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedged precedence claim. Same caveat as abstract — not mechanically resolvable. Hedge ('we have not located') accepted.

sentences.quote: Our scope choice is informed by the deep-research landscape review (Lit-Search/Phase-5t, December~2025), which identified a three-layer formalization hierarchy: Layer~1 (explicit doublon matrix computation), Layer~2 ($\mathbb{Z}_2$ Berry-phase quantization theorem for real-symmetric matrices), and Layer~3 (discrete Berry phase gauge-invariance).
sentences.quote_normalized: our scope choice is informed by the deep-research landscape review (lit-search/phase-5t, december~2025), which identified a three-layer formalization hierarchy: layer~1 (explicit doublon matrix computation), layer~2 ($\mathbb{z}_2$ berry-phase quantization theorem for real-symmetric matrices), and layer~3 (discrete berry phase gauge-invariance).
sentences.agent_notes: Internal-research provenance statement.

sentences.quote: This work completes Layer~1 with substantial Layer~2 quantitative scaling content, and leaves the full Berry-connection holonomy formalization (Target~B in our internal roadmap) for future work.
sentences.quote_normalized: this work completes layer~1 with substantial layer~2 quantitative scaling content, and leaves the full berry-connection holonomy formalization (target~b in our internal roadmap) for future work.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope summary; consistent with Section 6.

sentences.quote: (i)~A Lean~4 formalization of the Fermi-Hubbard dimer singlet-sector spectrum, covering the $3\times 3$ charge-conjugation Hamiltonian at zero detuning, the exact dark state, and the bright-state eigenvectors.
sentences.quote_normalized: (i)~a lean~4 formalization of the fermi-hubbard dimer singlet-sector spectrum, covering the $3\times 3$ charge-conjugation hamiltonian at zero detuning, the exact dark state, and the bright-state eigenvectors.

sentences.quote: (ii)~A formal proof (to our knowledge the first in any interactive proof assistant) of the discrete-step statement underlying the Kiefer \textit{et~al.} SWAP gate: $\darkstate(\theta + \pi) = -\darkstate(\theta)$ with zero dynamical phase.
sentences.quote_normalized: (ii)~a formal proof (to our knowledge the first in any interactive proof assistant) of the discrete-step statement underlying the kiefer et~al. swap gate: $\darkstate(\theta + \pi) = -\darkstate(\theta)$ with zero dynamical phase.
sentences.agent_notes: Both component theorems present. 'First in any IPA' is a hedged precedence claim ('to our knowledge').

sentences.quote: (iii)~A Mathlib-native construction of the SWAP operator as a real Householder reflection on \texttt{EuclideanSpace}, with canonical-form unitarity via \texttt{Matrix.unitaryGroup}.
sentences.quote_normalized: (iii)~a mathlib-native construction of the swap operator as a real householder reflection on euclideanspace, with canonical-form unitarity via matrix.unitarygroup.

sentences.quote: (iv)~A quantitative direct-exchange-vs-superexchange scaling theorem with explicit remainder bound $|J(t,U) - 4t^2/U| \le 16t^4/U^3$ for $U \ge 4|t| > 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: (iv)~a quantitative direct-exchange-vs-superexchange scaling theorem with explicit remainder bound $|j(t,u) - 4t^2/u| \le 16t^4/u^3$ for $u \ge 4|t| > 0$.
sentences.agent_notes: Theorem hypothesis (4|t| ≤ U) and conclusion match prose exactly.

sentences.quote: (v)~A Python cross-validation layer (\texttt{src/experimental/doublon\_gate.py}, \totaltests{}~project tests) exercising every Lean theorem against random parameter grids and numpy exact diagonalization.
sentences.quote_normalized: (v)~a python cross-validation layer (src/experimental/doublon\_gate.py, \totaltests{}~project tests) exercising every lean theorem against random parameter grids and numpy exact diagonalization.
sentences.agent_notes: \totaltests = 2884 (counts.tex). Phrase '\totaltests~project tests' is project-wide test count — not the per-paper-18 cross-validation count. Slightly loose but technically PASS (the macro is canonical).

sentences.quote: The gate's \emph{adiabatic} interpretation---the mapping from a closed loop in continuous parameter space to the discrete-step statement we prove---is a separate layer of the argument (Kato's adiabatic theorem, Berry's geometric phase as a connection 1-form).
sentences.quote_normalized: the gate's adiabatic interpretation---the mapping from a closed loop in continuous parameter space to the discrete-step statement we prove---is a separate layer of the argument (kato's adiabatic theorem, berry's geometric phase as a connection 1-form).
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-boundary disclosure.

sentences.quote: None of that machinery exists in current Mathlib.
sentences.quote_normalized: none of that machinery exists in current mathlib.
sentences.agent_notes: Mathlib state assertion (true at pinned rev 8850ed93).

sentences.quote: Our claim is strictly the finite-dimensional algebraic core: under the assumption that the continuous dynamics reduces to the discrete dark-state evolution (an assumption verified in~\cite{Kiefer2026} experimentally and, in general, by Kato's theorem), the gate action is exactly SWAP.
sentences.quote_normalized: our claim is strictly the finite-dimensional algebraic core: under the assumption that the continuous dynamics reduces to the discrete dark-state evolution (an assumption verified in~ experimentally and, in general, by kato's theorem), the gate action is exactly swap.
sentences.agent_notes: Hypothesis-disclosure-style scope statement; the externalized assumption is named in prose.

sentences.quote: The two-site Fermi-Hubbard dimer at half filling has a six-dimensional two-particle Hilbert space.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two-site fermi-hubbard dimer at half filling has a six-dimensional two-particle hilbert space.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Fermi-Hubbard combinatorics statement.

sentences.quote: We work in the site basis $\{\ket{\uparrow\uparrow}, \ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow, 0}, \ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow,\uparrow}, \ket{0, \uparrow\!\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow\downarrow}\}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: we work in the site basis $\{\ket{\uparrow\uparrow}, \ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow, 0}, \ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow,\uparrow}, \ket{0, \uparrow\!\downarrow}, \ket{\downarrow\downarrow}\}$.
sentences.agent_notes: Basis-choice declaration.

sentences.quote: Fermionic anti-symmetry (Pauli exclusion + hopping sign conventions) gives the full Hamiltonian~\cite{Kiefer2026} [equation block].
sentences.quote_normalized: fermionic anti-symmetry (pauli exclusion + hopping sign conventions) gives the full hamiltonian~ [equation block].
sentences.agent_notes: The 6x6 Hamiltonian is defined in Lean as Matrix (Fin 6) (Fin 6) ℝ; Kiefer2026 cited.

sentences.quote: where $t$ is the tunnel coupling, $\Delta$ is the inter-well detuning, and $U$ is the on-site Hubbard repulsion.
sentences.quote_normalized: where $t$ is the tunnel coupling, $\delta$ is the inter-well detuning, and $u$ is the on-site hubbard repulsion.
sentences.agent_notes: Variable definitions.

sentences.quote: The triplet sector $\{\ket{\uparrow\uparrow}, (\ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}+\ket{\downarrow,\uparrow})/\sqrt{2}, \ket{\downarrow\downarrow}\}$ decouples at zero energy for all parameters (Pauli exclusion + anti-symmetric hopping cancellation), leaving the nontrivial dynamics in the singlet sector.
sentences.quote_normalized: the triplet sector $\{\ket{\uparrow\uparrow}, (\ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}+\ket{\downarrow,\uparrow})/\sqrt{2}, \ket{\downarrow\downarrow}\}$ decouples at zero energy for all parameters (pauli exclusion + anti-symmetric hopping cancellation), leaving the nontrivial dynamics in the singlet sector.
sentences.agent_notes: T10a/T10b/T10c triplet zero theorems exist (lines 187/199/209 per prior review).

sentences.quote: In the symmetry-adapted singlet basis $\{\Dplus, \Dminus, \sstate\}$ where $\Dplus = (\ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow,0}+\ket{0,\uparrow\!\downarrow})/\sqrt{2}$, $\Dminus = (\ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow,0}-\ket{0,\uparrow\!\downarrow})/\sqrt{2}$, $\sstate = (\ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}-\ket{\downarrow,\uparrow})/\sqrt{2}$, the reduced Hamiltonian is [equation block].
sentences.quote_normalized: in the symmetry-adapted singlet basis $\{\dplus, \dminus, \sstate\}$ where $\dplus = (\ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow,0}+\ket{0,\uparrow\!\downarrow})/\sqrt{2}$, $\dminus = (\ket{\uparrow\!\downarrow,0}-\ket{0,\uparrow\!\downarrow})/\sqrt{2}$, $\sstate = (\ket{\uparrow,\downarrow}-\ket{\downarrow,\uparrow})/\sqrt{2}$, the reduced hamiltonian is [equation block].
sentences.agent_notes: 3x3 H_singlet matrix matches Lean def at line 112+ region.

sentences.quote: The gate operates at $U = 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gate operates at $u = 0$.
sentences.agent_notes: Configuration statement.

sentences.quote: At $\Delta = 0$, $\Dminus$ decouples from the rest of the singlet block, leaving a 2$\times$2 symmetric sector whose charpoly is exactly $\lambda^2 - U\lambda - 4t^2$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at $\delta = 0$, $\dminus$ decouples from the rest of the singlet block, leaving a 2$\times$2 symmetric sector whose charpoly is exactly $\lambda^2 - u\lambda - 4t^2$.
sentences.agent_notes: Lean has charpoly_H_singlet (W4) and charpoly_H_singlet_Δ0_factored (line 2115) which gives (X-U)(X^2-UX-4t^2).

sentences.quote: The 3$\times$3 and 6$\times$6 Hamiltonians are defined as explicit \texttt{Matrix (Fin~3) (Fin~3)~$\mathbb{R}$} and \texttt{Matrix (Fin~6) (Fin~6)~$\mathbb{R}$} values.
sentences.quote_normalized: the 3$\times$3 and 6$\times$6 hamiltonians are defined as explicit \texttt{matrix (fin~3) (fin~3)~$\mathbb{r}$} and \texttt{matrix (fin~6) (fin~6)~$\mathbb{r}$} values.
sentences.agent_notes: Description of Lean encoding.

sentences.quote: The seven Layer-1 core theorems (T1--T7) establish real-symmetry, dark-state kernel-membership at $U=0$, chiral involution $\Gamma^2 = I$, chiral anti-commutation $\{\Gamma, H_{\rm singlet}\} = 0$ at $U=0$, and the explicit determinant and trace identities.
sentences.quote_normalized: the seven layer-1 core theorems (t1--t7) establish real-symmetry, dark-state kernel-membership at $u=0$, chiral involution $\gamma^2 = i$, chiral anti-commutation $\{\gamma, h_{\rm singlet}\} = 0$ at $u=0$, and the explicit determinant and trace identities.
sentences.agent_notes: T1-T7 ascertained against lean_deps; det/trace theorems also verified.

sentences.quote: The triplet zero-energy theorems (T10a--c) use \texttt{fin\_cases + Fin.sum\_univ\_six + simp}, closing in milliseconds.
sentences.quote_normalized: the triplet zero-energy theorems (t10a--c) use fin\_cases + fin.sum\_univ\_six + simp, closing in milliseconds.
sentences.agent_notes: Tactic provenance description.

sentences.quote: The Hamiltonian~(\ref{eq:Hsinglet}) at $U = 0$ belongs to the BDI symmetry class of the tenfold way~\cite{Kitaev2009,AltlandZirnbauer}: it is real symmetric (time-reversal trivial) and anti-commutes with the chiral operator $\Gamma = \mathrm{diag}(+1, -1, -1)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the hamiltonian~() at $u = 0$ belongs to the bdi symmetry class of the tenfold way~: it is real symmetric (time-reversal trivial) and anti-commutes with the chiral operator $\gamma = diag(+1, -1, -1)$.

sentences.quote: The BDI structure \textbf{pins an odd-dimensional sector's spectrum to contain zero:} in an odd-dimensional real symmetric matrix anti-commuting with a chiral involution, the nonzero eigenvalues pair as $\{+E, -E\}$, leaving one unpaired zero.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bdi structure pins an odd-dimensional sector's spectrum to contain zero: in an odd-dimensional real symmetric matrix anti-commuting with a chiral involution, the nonzero eigenvalues pair as $\{+e, -e\}$, leaving one unpaired zero.

sentences.quote: The unnormalized dark-state vector is $\ket{\text{dark}(t, \Delta)} = (0, 2t, \Delta)^T \in \mathrm{ker}(H_{\rm singlet}(t, \Delta, 0))$, with norm $\|\text{dark}\|_2 = \sqrt{\Delta^2 + 4t^2}$---the energy gap to the two bright states $\ket{\rm bright\pm}$ with eigenvalues $\pm\sqrt{\Delta^2 + 4t^2}$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the unnormalized dark-state vector is $\ket{dark(t, \delta)} = (0, 2t, \delta)^t \in ker(h_{\rm singlet}(t, \delta, 0))$, with norm $\|dark\|_2 = \sqrt{\delta^2 + 4t^2}$---the energy gap to the two bright states $\ket{\rm bright\pm}$ with eigenvalues $\pm\sqrt{\delta^2 + 4t^2}$.

sentences.quote: We formalize: (i)~$\Gamma\cdot H\cdot\Gamma = -H$ at $U=0$ (W5a); (ii)~the spectral-pairing mechanism $H v = E v \Rightarrow H (\Gamma v) = -E (\Gamma v)$ (W5b); (iii)~the multiset spectrum pairing at $U=0$ (W5k); (iv)~the dark-state kernel-invariance under $\Gamma$ (W5i); (v)~the full chiral-projector algebra $P_\pm = (1\pm\Gamma)/2$ with idempotency, orthogonality, completeness, and $\Gamma$-action $\pm 1$ on the respective eigenspaces (W5l--W5o2); (vi)~the \emph{zero-mode uniqueness} theorem (W5p): $H_{\rm singlet}(t, \Delta, 0)\cdot v = 0 \Rightarrow \exists c \in \mathbb{R},\ v = c\cdot\ket{\text{dark}(t,\Delta)}$ provided $(t, \Delta) \ne (0, 0)$---this is the strongest ``pinning'' statement: the kernel is \emph{exactly} $\mathbb{R}\cdot\ket{\text{dark}}$, not merely $\Gamma$-invariant.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize: (i)~$\gamma\cdot h\cdot\gamma = -h$ at $u=0$ (w5a); (ii)~the spectral-pairing mechanism $h v = e v \rightarrow h (\gamma v) = -e (\gamma v)$ (w5b); (iii)~the multiset spectrum pairing at $u=0$ (w5k); (iv)~the dark-state kernel-invariance under $\gamma$ (w5i); (v)~the full chiral-projector algebra $p_\pm = (1\pm\gamma)/2$ with idempotency, orthogonality, completeness, and $\gamma$-action $\pm 1$ on the respective eigenspaces (w5l--w5o2); (vi)~the zero-mode uniqueness theorem (w5p): $h_{\rm singlet}(t, \delta, 0)\cdot v = 0 \rightarrow \exists c \in \mathbb{r},\ v = c\cdot\ket{dark(t,\delta)}$ provided $(t, \delta) \ne (0, 0)$---this is the strongest ``pinning'' statement: the kernel is exactly $\mathbb{r}\cdot\ket{dark}$, not merely $\gamma$-invariant.
sentences.agent_notes: Six-fold conjunctive formal claim; all named theorems (W5a/b/k/i/l-o2/p) are present. \fhdWaveFive macro = 22 in counts.tex.

sentences.quote: With the dark state in hand, the geometric SWAP operator on the singlet sector is $\USWAP(t, \Delta) = I - \frac{2}{\Delta^2 + 4t^2}\,\ket{\text{dark}}\bra{\text{dark}}$---a real Householder reflection.
sentences.quote_normalized: with the dark state in hand, the geometric swap operator on the singlet sector is $\uswap(t, \delta) = i - \frac{2}{\delta^2 + 4t^2}\,\ket{dark}\bra{dark}$---a real householder reflection.
sentences.agent_notes: U_SWAP_singlet at line 1555 of FermiHubbardDimer.lean; matches the Lean def: 1 - (2/(Δ^2+4t^2)) • |d><d|.

sentences.quote: By construction it acts as $-1$ on the dark direction and as $+1$ on the two-dimensional bright subspace orthogonal to it.
sentences.quote_normalized: by construction it acts as $-1$ on the dark direction and as $+1$ on the two-dimensional bright subspace orthogonal to it.

sentences.quote: The key design decision was the \emph{Path~A} re-typing: we lift the eigenvectors from bare \texttt{Fin~3~$\to$~$\mathbb{R}$} (default sup-norm) to \texttt{EuclideanSpace~$\mathbb{R}$~(Fin~3)} (L${}^2$ norm) via \texttt{EuclideanSpace.equiv.symm}, then build the \texttt{OrthonormalBasis} via Mathlib's \texttt{basisOfOrthonormalOfCardEqFinrank}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the key design decision was the path~a re-typing: we lift the eigenvectors from bare \texttt{fin~3~$\to$~$\mathbb{r}$} (default sup-norm) to \texttt{euclideanspace~$\mathbb{r}$~(fin~3)} (l${}^2$ norm) via euclideanspace.equiv.symm, then build the orthonormalbasis via mathlib's basisoforthonormalofcardeqfinrank.
sentences.agent_notes: Implementation methodology.

sentences.quote: This unlocks the one-line unitarity proof \texttt{OrthonormalBasis.toMatrix\_orthonormalBasis\_mem\_unitary} for the change-of-basis matrix between \texttt{EuclideanSpace.basisFun} and the eigenbasis.
sentences.quote_normalized: this unlocks the one-line unitarity proof orthonormalbasis.tomatrix\_orthonormalbasis\_mem\_unitary for the change-of-basis matrix between euclideanspace.basisfun and the eigenbasis.

sentences.quote: The SWAP operator itself inherits symmetry, involution, and orthogonality from the Householder algebra: with $P = \ket{\rm dark}\bra{\rm dark}/\text{gap}^2$ idempotent ($P^2 = P$), $(I - 2P)^2 = I - 4P + 4P^2 = I$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the swap operator itself inherits symmetry, involution, and orthogonality from the householder algebra: with $p = \ket{\rm dark}\bra{\rm dark}/gap^2$ idempotent ($p^2 = p$), $(i - 2p)^2 = i - 4p + 4p^2 = i$.
sentences.agent_notes: U_SWAP_singlet_sq (W6C-S2: U^2 = I) at line 1626 in Lean.

sentences.quote: The canonical-Mathlib unitary-group membership $\USWAP \in \mathrm{Matrix.unitaryGroup~(Fin~3)~}\mathbb{R}$ (W6C-U1) is a one-line corollary via \texttt{Matrix.mem\_unitaryGroup\_iff'} and \texttt{Matrix.conjTranspose\_eq\_transpose\_of\_trivial} (the \texttt{TrivialStar}~$\mathbb{R}$ instance reduces $\overline{\USWAP}^{\mathsf{T}}$ to $\USWAP^{\mathsf{T}}$ over $\mathbb{R}$).
sentences.quote_normalized: the canonical-mathlib unitary-group membership $\uswap \in matrix.unitarygroup~(fin~3)~\mathbb{r}$ (w6c-u1) is a one-line corollary via matrix.mem\_unitarygroup\_iff' and matrix.conjtranspose\_eq\_transpose\_of\_trivial (the trivialstar~$\mathbb{r}$ instance reduces $\overline{\uswap}^{\mathsf{t}}$ to $\uswap^{\mathsf{t}}$ over $\mathbb{r}$).

sentences.quote: The singlet-basis SWAP operator extends to a $6\times 6$ block-diagonal operator on the full symmetry-adapted basis via \texttt{Matrix.fromBlocks (U\_SWAP\_singlet) 0 0 1}, with the identity block acting on the decoupled triplet sector.
sentences.quote_normalized: the singlet-basis swap operator extends to a $6\times 6$ block-diagonal operator on the full symmetry-adapted basis via matrix.fromblocks (u\_swap\_singlet) 0 0 1, with the identity block acting on the decoupled triplet sector.
sentences.agent_notes: 6x6 lift via U_SWAP_adapted_* family (lines 2317-2379 per prior review).

sentences.quote: The $6\times 6$ unitarity is inherited from W6C via \texttt{fromBlocks\_multiply} and \texttt{fromBlocks\_transpose}---there is no new physics here, merely the structural statement that the gate preserves the triplet sector identically.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $6\times 6$ unitarity is inherited from w6c via fromblocks\_multiply and fromblocks\_transpose---there is no new physics here, merely the structural statement that the gate preserves the triplet sector identically.
sentences.agent_notes: 6x6 unitarity inheritance.

sentences.quote: The kernel-action theorem (W6C-K1) combines zero-mode uniqueness with the sign flip: for any $v$ in the kernel of $H_{\rm singlet}(t,\Delta,0)$ with $(t,\Delta) \ne (0,0)$, $\USWAP\cdot v = -v$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the kernel-action theorem (w6c-k1) combines zero-mode uniqueness with the sign flip: for any $v$ in the kernel of $h_{\rm singlet}(t,\delta,0)$ with $(t,\delta) \ne (0,0)$, $\uswap\cdot v = -v$.

sentences.quote: This is the strongest ``SWAP acts as $-1$ on the pinned zero-mode subspace'' claim in the formalization.
sentences.quote_normalized: this is the strongest ``swap acts as $-1$ on the pinned zero-mode subspace'' claim in the formalization.
sentences.agent_notes: Self-summary qualitative claim.

sentences.quote: Beyond the gate-action theorem, we ship the quantitative scaling distinction between the direct-exchange regime ($U$ small) and the superexchange regime ($U$ large).
sentences.quote_normalized: beyond the gate-action theorem, we ship the quantitative scaling distinction between the direct-exchange regime ($u$ small) and the superexchange regime ($u$ large).

sentences.quote: At $\Delta = 0$, the 2$\times$2 symmetric sector has explicit eigenvalues $E_\pm(t, U) = \frac{U \pm \sqrt{U^2 + 16 t^2}}{2}$, roots of $\lambda^2 - U\lambda - 4t^2 = 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: at $\delta = 0$, the 2$\times$2 symmetric sector has explicit eigenvalues $e_\pm(t, u) = \frac{u \pm \sqrt{u^2 + 16 t^2}}{2}$, roots of $\lambda^2 - u\lambda - 4t^2 = 0$.

sentences.quote: The direct-exchange value is $E_+(t, 0) = 2|t|$ (linear in hopping); the superexchange gap $J(t, U) := E_+ - U = (\sqrt{U^2 + 16t^2} - U)/2$ approaches $4t^2/U$ at large $U$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the direct-exchange value is $e_+(t, 0) = 2|t|$ (linear in hopping); the superexchange gap $j(t, u) := e_+ - u = (\sqrt{u^2 + 16t^2} - u)/2$ approaches $4t^2/u$ at large $u$.

sentences.quote: $E_+(t, 0) = 2|t|$ (W7a)---direct-exchange value at $U=0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: $e_+(t, 0) = 2|t|$ (w7a)---direct-exchange value at $u=0$.

sentences.quote: Vieta identities $E_+ + E_- = U$ (W7b) and $E_+\cdot E_- = -4t^2$ (W7c)---match the 2$\times$2 trace and determinant of the symmetric-sector block.
sentences.quote_normalized: vieta identities $e_+ + e_- = u$ (w7b) and $e_+\cdot e_- = -4t^2$ (w7c)---match the 2$\times$2 trace and determinant of the symmetric-sector block.

sentences.quote: $\text{HasDerivAt}\,(E_+(t,\cdot))\,(1/2)\,0$ for $t \ne 0$ (W7d)---\textbf{the direct-exchange linear-in-$U$ scaling theorem}.
sentences.quote_normalized: $hasderivat\,(e_+(t,\cdot))\,(1/2)\,0$ for $t \ne 0$ (w7d)---the direct-exchange linear-in-$u$ scaling theorem.

sentences.quote: The Taylor coefficient at $U = 0$ is exactly $1/2$, proved via the Mathlib \texttt{HasDerivAt.sqrt / add / div\_const} chain.
sentences.quote_normalized: the taylor coefficient at $u = 0$ is exactly $1/2$, proved via the mathlib hasderivat.sqrt / add / div\_const chain.

sentences.quote: $|E_+(t, U_1) - E_+(t, U_2)| \le |U_1 - U_2|$ (W7q)---the Tier-1 global 1-Lipschitz bound per the deep-research landscape review.
sentences.quote_normalized: $|e_+(t, u_1) - e_+(t, u_2)| \le |u_1 - u_2|$ (w7q)---the tier-1 global 1-lipschitz bound per the deep-research landscape review.

sentences.quote: $|J(t, U) - 4t^2/U| \le 16t^4/U^3$ for $0 < 4|t| \le U$ (W7i)---\textbf{the Tier-2 superexchange approximation theorem}.
sentences.quote_normalized: $|j(t, u) - 4t^2/u| \le 16t^4/u^3$ for $0 < 4|t| \le u$ (w7i)---the tier-2 superexchange approximation theorem.

sentences.quote: The proof chains the algebraic identity $J - 4t^2/U = -(s-U)^2/(4U)$ (where $s = \sqrt{U^2+16t^2}$) with the sqrt-bound $s \le U + 8t^2/U$, itself proved by squaring both sides.
sentences.quote_normalized: the proof chains the algebraic identity $j - 4t^2/u = -(s-u)^2/(4u)$ (where $s = \sqrt{u^2+16t^2}$) with the sqrt-bound $s \le u + 8t^2/u$, itself proved by squaring both sides.

sentences.quote: The matrix-bridge theorem $\mathrm{charpoly}(H_{\rm singlet}(t, 0, U)) = (X - U)(X^2 - UX - 4t^2)$ (W7l)---provides the full spectrum $\{U, E_+, E_-\}$ at $\Delta = 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the matrix-bridge theorem $charpoly(h_{\rm singlet}(t, 0, u)) = (x - u)(x^2 - ux - 4t^2)$ (w7l)---provides the full spectrum $\{u, e_+, e_-\}$ at $\delta = 0$.
sentences.agent_notes: Lean theorem at line 2115 (per prior review).

sentences.quote: Explicit eigenvectors $H_{\rm singlet}(t, 0, U)\cdot (E_\pm, 0, -2t)^\mathsf{T} = E_\pm\cdot(E_\pm, 0, -2t)^\mathsf{T}$ (W7m--n) and the antisymmetric doublon $\cdot(0, 1, 0)^\mathsf{T} = U\cdot(0, 1, 0)^\mathsf{T}$ (W7o).
sentences.quote_normalized: explicit eigenvectors $h_{\rm singlet}(t, 0, u)\cdot (e_\pm, 0, -2t)^\mathsf{t} = e_\pm\cdot(e_\pm, 0, -2t)^\mathsf{t}$ (w7m--n) and the antisymmetric doublon $\cdot(0, 1, 0)^\mathsf{t} = u\cdot(0, 1, 0)^\mathsf{t}$ (w7o).

sentences.quote: The sensitivity contrast recovered from these scalars: at $U = 0$, the fractional sensitivity $(\partial_t \Delta) \cdot (t/\Delta) = 1$ (a 1\% hopping fluctuation shifts the gap by 1\%); at large~$U$, the superexchange fractional sensitivity $(\partial_t J) \cdot (t/J) = 2$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sensitivity contrast recovered from these scalars: at $u = 0$, the fractional sensitivity $(\partial_t \delta) \cdot (t/\delta) = 1$ (a 1\% hopping fluctuation shifts the gap by 1\%); at large~$u$, the superexchange fractional sensitivity $(\partial_t j) \cdot (t/j) = 2$.
sentences.agent_notes: Sensitivity analysis recoverable from formalized scalar identities; not a formal Lean theorem itself but a derived corollary in the prose.

sentences.quote: This factor-of-2 difference is consistent with the observed $4\times$ robustness of the geometric gate relative to superexchange~\cite{Kiefer2026}; the algebraic factor of $2$ is one contribution to the experimental difference, not a complete derivation.
sentences.quote_normalized: this factor-of-2 difference is consistent with the observed $4\times$ robustness of the geometric gate relative to superexchange~; the algebraic factor of $2$ is one contribution to the experimental difference, not a complete derivation.
sentences.agent_notes: Prior v1 review (WARN) flagged this as overclaim ('underlies'). Current paper softens to 'is consistent with' + 'one contribution, not a complete derivation' — explicit hedge addresses prior concern. Prior WARN does NOT reproduce.

sentences.quote: Figure~\ref{fig:spectrum} shows the spectrum and the superexchange approximation bound, cross-verified by numpy exact diagonalization.
sentences.quote_normalized: figure~ shows the spectrum and the superexchange approximation bound, cross-verified by numpy exact diagonalization.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Left:} singlet-sector spectrum at $\Delta = 0$ versus $U$ (in units of $t = 1$). Three eigenvalues $\{U, E_+(t, U), E_-(t, U)\}$---the antisymmetric doublon (dashed, grey), $E_+$ (blue), and $E_-$ (amber). Markers are independent numpy \texttt{eigh} diagonalization cross-checking the Lean closed-form values of $E_\pm$ via the matrix-bridge theorem~W7l (the characteristic polynomial factorization). \textbf{Right:} superexchange gap $|J(t, U)|$ on log--log axes, with the $4t^2/U$ asymptote (dashed) and the Lean~W7i error envelope $\pm 16 t^4/U^3$ shaded. At $U = 4|t|$ the bound is tightest; the envelope widens toward the asymptote as $U \to \infty$.
sentences.quote_normalized: left: singlet-sector spectrum at $\delta = 0$ versus $u$ (in units of $t = 1$). three eigenvalues $\{u, e_+(t, u), e_-(t, u)\}$---the antisymmetric doublon (dashed, grey), $e_+$ (blue), and $e_-$ (amber). markers are independent numpy eigh diagonalization cross-checking the lean closed-form values of $e_\pm$ via the matrix-bridge theorem~w7l (the characteristic polynomial factorization). right: superexchange gap $|j(t, u)|$ on log--log axes, with the $4t^2/u$ asymptote (dashed) and the lean~w7i error envelope $\pm 16 t^4/u^3$ shaded. at $u = 4|t|$ the bound is tightest; the envelope widens toward the asymptote as $u \to \infty$.
sentences.agent_notes: Figure caption; describes the figure in fig_doublon_gate_spectrum.png. Single record (caption-level).

sentences.quote: The gate's geometric interpretation rests on two independent finite-dimensional facts: a \emph{sign flip} on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle, and \emph{vanishing dynamical phase} along the zero-energy dark-state path.
sentences.quote_normalized: the gate's geometric interpretation rests on two independent finite-dimensional facts: a sign flip on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle, and vanishing dynamical phase along the zero-energy dark-state path.

sentences.quote: Together these are the two necessary conditions for interpreting any accumulated phase along a closed loop as purely geometric; assuming the adiabatic theorem supplies the dynamics (i.e.\ a continuous path along the eigenbundle), the accumulated phase is then forced to be exactly $-1$.
sentences.quote_normalized: together these are the two necessary conditions for interpreting any accumulated phase along a closed loop as purely geometric; assuming the adiabatic theorem supplies the dynamics (i.e.\ a continuous path along the eigenbundle), the accumulated phase is then forced to be exactly $-1$.
sentences.agent_notes: Conditional/scope statement; explicit hypothesis-disclosure language.

sentences.quote: We formalize the two necessary conditions; the adiabatic assumption is external.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize the two necessary conditions; the adiabatic assumption is external.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope disclosure.

sentences.quote: The $\theta$-parameterized dark state is $\ket{\text{dark}(\theta)} = (0, \sin\theta, \cos\theta)^\mathsf{T}$ in the $\{\Dplus, \Dminus, \sstate\}$ basis.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\theta$-parameterized dark state is $\ket{dark(\theta)} = (0, \sin\theta, \cos\theta)^\mathsf{t}$ in the $\{\dplus, \dminus, \sstate\}$ basis.

sentences.quote: When the parameters satisfy the kernel-angle condition $\Delta\sin\theta = 2t\cos\theta$ (equivalent to $\tan\theta = 2t/\Delta$), this vector is in the kernel of $H_{\rm singlet}(t, \Delta, 0)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: when the parameters satisfy the kernel-angle condition $\delta\sin\theta = 2t\cos\theta$ (equivalent to $\tan\theta = 2t/\delta$), this vector is in the kernel of $h_{\rm singlet}(t, \delta, 0)$.

sentences.quote: $\ket{\text{dark}(\theta + \pi)} = -\ket{\text{dark}(\theta)}$ (W8a)---\textbf{the closed-path sign statement}, direct from $\sin(\theta+\pi) = -\sin\theta$ and $\cos(\theta+\pi) = -\cos\theta$.
sentences.quote_normalized: $\ket{dark(\theta + \pi)} = -\ket{dark(\theta)}$ (w8a)---the closed-path sign statement, direct from $\sin(\theta+\pi) = -\sin\theta$ and $\cos(\theta+\pi) = -\cos\theta$.

sentences.quote: $\ket{\text{dark}(\theta + 2\pi)} = \ket{\text{dark}(\theta)}$ (W8b)---periodicity.
sentences.quote_normalized: $\ket{dark(\theta + 2\pi)} = \ket{dark(\theta)}$ (w8b)---periodicity.

sentences.quote: $\langle\text{dark}(\theta)\mid\text{dark}(\theta)\rangle = 1$ (W8c)---unit norm via Pythagorean identity.
sentences.quote_normalized: $\langledark(\theta)\middark(\theta)\rangle = 1$ (w8c)---unit norm via pythagorean identity.

sentences.quote: $\langle\text{dark}(\theta)\mid H_{\rm singlet}(t,\Delta,0)\cdot\text{dark}(\theta)\rangle = 0$ (W8d)---\textbf{dynamical phase vanishes}.
sentences.quote_normalized: $\langledark(\theta)\mid h_{\rm singlet}(t,\delta,0)\cdotdark(\theta)\rangle = 0$ (w8d)---dynamical phase vanishes.

sentences.quote: Direct corollary of the kernel membership W4a: $H\cdot\ket{\text{dark}} = 0$ pointwise along the $\theta$-path implies the integrated dynamical phase vanishes identically.
sentences.quote_normalized: direct corollary of the kernel membership w4a: $h\cdot\ket{dark} = 0$ pointwise along the $\theta$-path implies the integrated dynamical phase vanishes identically.

sentences.quote: Bundled: W8a~$\wedge$~W8d (W8e)---\textbf{the two necessary conditions} for a Berry-phase interpretation of the gate action.
sentences.quote_normalized: bundled: w8a~$\wedge$~w8d (w8e)---the two necessary conditions for a berry-phase interpretation of the gate action.

sentences.quote: The Lean theorem's conclusion is the conjunction itself, not an equality involving an integrated phase functional (no integral is constructed in the formalization).
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean theorem's conclusion is the conjunction itself, not an equality involving an integrated phase functional (no integral is constructed in the formalization).
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-honesty statement.

sentences.quote: Consumers who supply the adiabatic-theorem assumption can combine this conjunction with a separate dynamical argument to conclude the accumulated phase is $-1$; we do not formalize that last step here.
sentences.quote_normalized: consumers who supply the adiabatic-theorem assumption can combine this conjunction with a separate dynamical argument to conclude the accumulated phase is $-1$; we do not formalize that last step here.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-honesty.

sentences.quote: The $\pi$ shift rather than $2\pi$ reflects our full-angle parameterization convention $\ket{\text{dark}(\theta)} = (0, \sin\theta, \cos\theta)^\mathsf{T}$; the equivalent half-angle convention of the physics literature maps a $2\pi$ lattice cycle to a $\pi$ mixing-angle sweep, giving the same final result.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\pi$ shift rather than $2\pi$ reflects our full-angle parameterization convention $\ket{dark(\theta)} = (0, \sin\theta, \cos\theta)^\mathsf{t}$; the equivalent half-angle convention of the physics literature maps a $2\pi$ lattice cycle to a $\pi$ mixing-angle sweep, giving the same final result.

sentences.quote: W8e is the \emph{discrete-step} statement underlying the gate: a $\pi$ sweep in the mixing angle inverts the dark state.
sentences.quote_normalized: w8e is the discrete-step statement underlying the gate: a $\pi$ sweep in the mixing angle inverts the dark state.

sentences.quote: The \emph{continuous} interpretation---that the physical control-pulse sequence in~\cite{Kiefer2026} drives the dark state smoothly along this trajectory because of the adiabatic theorem---is not proved here.
sentences.quote_normalized: the continuous interpretation---that the physical control-pulse sequence in~ drives the dark state smoothly along this trajectory because of the adiabatic theorem---is not proved here.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-boundary statement.

sentences.quote: It enters as a physics assumption, verified experimentally in~\cite{Kiefer2026} and, in general, by Kato's theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: it enters as a physics assumption, verified experimentally in~ and, in general, by kato's theorem.

sentences.quote: The full Berry-connection holonomy picture (a 1-form on a parameterized eigenbundle whose integral around the loop equals our $-1$) likewise requires machinery that is not yet in Mathlib: continuous parameterized eigenvectors, connections on vector bundles, parallel transport.
sentences.quote_normalized: the full berry-connection holonomy picture (a 1-form on a parameterized eigenbundle whose integral around the loop equals our $-1$) likewise requires machinery that is not yet in mathlib: continuous parameterized eigenvectors, connections on vector bundles, parallel transport.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope-boundary; matches Mathlib state.

sentences.quote_normalized: those are target~b of our internal roadmap; see section~.

sentences.quote: No ordinary differential equations, no functional analysis, no differential geometry.
sentences.quote_normalized: no ordinary differential equations, no functional analysis, no differential geometry.

sentences.quote: The deliberate scope boundary of this work is Layer~1+2 of the deep-research target hierarchy.
sentences.quote_normalized: the deliberate scope boundary of this work is layer~1+2 of the deep-research target hierarchy.

sentences.quote: Three genuinely new results could extend it:
sentences.quote_normalized: three genuinely new results could extend it:

sentences.quote: The target theorem (not proved here): \textit{for a continuous loop $H: [0,1] \to \mathrm{Sym}_n(\mathbb{R})$ with $H(0) = H(1)$, a simple eigenvalue $\lambda(s)$ along the loop, and a continuous unit eigenvector $v(s)$, the return sign $\langle v(0), v(1)\rangle \in \{+1, -1\}$}---a pure linear-algebra statement expressing the first Stiefel-Whitney class of the real eigenvector bundle over $S^1$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the target theorem (not proved here): \textit{for a continuous loop $h: [0,1] \to sym_n(\mathbb{r})$ with $h(0) = h(1)$, a simple eigenvalue $\lambda(s)$ along the loop, and a continuous unit eigenvector $v(s)$, the return sign $\langle v(0), v(1)\rangle \in \{+1, -1\}$}---a pure linear-algebra statement expressing the first stiefel-whitney class of the real eigenvector bundle over $s^1$.
sentences.agent_notes: Future-work statement; correctly disclaims as not proved.

sentences.quote: Our estimate is 2--3 weeks; the main obstacle is the continuous-eigenvector construction from Mathlib's \texttt{IsHermitian.spectral\_theorem}, which has no parameterized smoothness guarantee out of the box.
sentences.quote_normalized: our estimate is 2--3 weeks; the main obstacle is the continuous-eigenvector construction from mathlib's ishermitian.spectral\_theorem, which has no parameterized smoothness guarantee out of the box.

sentences.quote: Would be the first formal $\mathbb{Z}_2$ Berry-phase theorem in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: would be the first formal $\mathbb{z}_2$ berry-phase theorem in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedged-future precedence claim; not mechanically resolvable.

sentences.quote: Define the discrete Berry phase for a finite sequence of real unit vectors as $\mathrm{sign}(\prod_i \langle v_i | v_{i+1\mod N}\rangle)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: define the discrete berry phase for a finite sequence of real unit vectors as $sign(\prod_i \langle v_i | v_{i+1\mod n}\rangle)$.

sentences.quote: Prove gauge invariance (flipping any $v_k \to -v_k$ preserves the product) and the continuous-limit equivalence to Layer~2.
sentences.quote_normalized: prove gauge invariance (flipping any $v_k \to -v_k$ preserves the product) and the continuous-limit equivalence to layer~2.

sentences.quote: Connects to the Pancharatnam-Bargmann formulation.
sentences.quote_normalized: connects to the pancharatnam-bargmann formulation.

sentences.quote: Another 2--3 weeks.
sentences.quote_normalized: another 2--3 weeks.

sentences.quote: The dynamical-side companion to Layer~2: a continuous time evolution whose state-space projection onto the eigenbundle converges to parallel transport as the sweep rate goes to zero.
sentences.quote_normalized: the dynamical-side companion to layer~2: a continuous time evolution whose state-space projection onto the eigenbundle converges to parallel transport as the sweep rate goes to zero.

sentences.quote: Requires Picard-Lindel\"of ODE existence (exists in Mathlib), matrix exponentials (exists), and the adiabatic error bound itself (does not exist in any form).
sentences.quote_normalized: requires picard-lindel\"of ode existence (exists in mathlib), matrix exponentials (exists), and the adiabatic error bound itself (does not exist in any form).

sentences.quote: Months of work; not recommended as Phase~5t/Phase~6 scope per our roadmap.
sentences.quote_normalized: months of work; not recommended as phase~5t/phase~6 scope per our roadmap.

sentences.quote: We have formalized the algebraic core of the Kiefer~\textit{et~al.} doublon geometric SWAP gate end-to-end in Lean~4, reducing the gate's \emph{geometric phase} to the observation that $\cos(\pi) = -1$ composed with an explicit Householder reflection on a parameterized $3\times 3$ real symmetric matrix.
sentences.quote_normalized: we have formalized the algebraic core of the kiefer~et~al. doublon geometric swap gate end-to-end in lean~4, reducing the gate's geometric phase to the observation that $\cos(\pi) = -1$ composed with an explicit householder reflection on a parameterized $3\times 3$ real symmetric matrix.

sentences.quote: The key algebraic facts are: the BDI-pinned zero-energy dark state; the SWAP operator as $I - 2P$ with $P$ a rank-1 projector onto the dark direction; the Vieta/characteristic-equation matrix-bridge tying the scalar eigenvalue formulas $E_\pm(t, U)$ back to the full Hamiltonian spectrum; the Tier-2 superexchange approximation bound with explicit $16 t^4/U^3$ remainder; and the minimum Berry-phase statement, that the dark state acquires $-1$ on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle while remaining at zero energy throughout.
sentences.quote_normalized: the key algebraic facts are: the bdi-pinned zero-energy dark state; the swap operator as $i - 2p$ with $p$ a rank-1 projector onto the dark direction; the vieta/characteristic-equation matrix-bridge tying the scalar eigenvalue formulas $e_\pm(t, u)$ back to the full hamiltonian spectrum; the tier-2 superexchange approximation bound with explicit $16 t^4/u^3$ remainder; and the minimum berry-phase statement, that the dark state acquires $-1$ on a $\pi$-sweep of the mixing angle while remaining at zero energy throughout.

sentences.quote: The scope discipline---no adiabatic theorem, no Berry connection, no fiber bundles---was informed by an explicit landscape review of the Mathlib dependency graph.
sentences.quote_normalized: the scope discipline---no adiabatic theorem, no berry connection, no fiber bundles---was informed by an explicit landscape review of the mathlib dependency graph.

sentences.quote: The result is a formalization that Mathlib's existing \texttt{Matrix}, \texttt{EuclideanSpace}, \texttt{OrthonormalBasis}, and \texttt{Polynomial} APIs are directly equipped to handle, without the need to develop new infrastructure for differential geometry or functional analysis.
sentences.quote_normalized: the result is a formalization that mathlib's existing matrix, euclideanspace, orthonormalbasis, and polynomial apis are directly equipped to handle, without the need to develop new infrastructure for differential geometry or functional analysis.

sentences.quote: The $\sim 2{,}400$ lines of Lean source contain \fhdTotal{}~theorems in a single module, zero sorry, zero new axioms, covering the full gate story from fermion Hilbert space decomposition to Berry phase.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $\sim 2{,}400$ lines of lean source contain \fhdtotal{}~theorems in a single module, zero sorry, zero new axioms, covering the full gate story from fermion hilbert space decomposition to berry phase.
sentences.agent_notes: '~2,400 lines' against actual 2557 lines is 6.1% delta (above 0.5% but the sentence uses 'approx' which signals soft-rounded). Earlier in Section 7, paper says '2,540 lines' (accurate). Inconsistency between two sentences in the same paper triggers Class IA WARN. Recommend rounding both to 2,540 or '~2,500'.

sentences.quote: The natural next target is Layer~2 of the deep-research hierarchy: the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ Berry-phase quantization theorem for arbitrary continuous loops of real-symmetric matrices with a simple eigenvalue.
sentences.quote_normalized: the natural next target is layer~2 of the deep-research hierarchy: the $\mathbb{z}_2$ berry-phase quantization theorem for arbitrary continuous loops of real-symmetric matrices with a simple eigenvalue.

sentences.quote: A successful formalization would be the first $\mathbb{Z}_2$ Berry-phase result in any interactive proof assistant, and would complete the \emph{kinematic} Berry-phase story without touching the adiabatic theorem or any ODE/PDE machinery.
sentences.quote_normalized: a successful formalization would be the first $\mathbb{z}_2$ berry-phase result in any interactive proof assistant, and would complete the kinematic berry-phase story without touching the adiabatic theorem or any ode/pde machinery.
sentences.agent_notes: Hedged future precedence claim.

sentences.quote: This work builds on the Mathlib library and the Lean~4 proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: this work builds on the mathlib library and the lean~4 proof assistant.

sentences.quote: We acknowledge the Aristotle automated theorem prover for filling a small number of finite-combinatorial gaps (zero in this paper; see the full-project provenance in \texttt{SK\_EFT\_Hawking\_Inventory.md}).
sentences.quote_normalized: we acknowledge the aristotle automated theorem prover for filling a small number of finite-combinatorial gaps (zero in this paper; see the full-project provenance in sk\_eft\_hawking\_inventory.md).
sentences.agent_notes: Aristotle attribution; correctly states 'zero in this paper'.
